agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "Digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer and outbound money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.",
    "bounded_context": "Wallet Transfers & Account Compliance",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "KYC (Know Your Customer)", "definition": "Identity verification process required before a user is authorized to send money or withdraw funds. A regulatory obligation, not a product preference." },
      { "term": "Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed full KYC. Authorized to send, receive, and withdraw funds. Subject to a $5,000 daily transfer limit." },
      { "term": "Partially-Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. Can send money subject to a $500 daily transfer limit. Can receive money. Cannot withdraw." },
      { "term": "Unverified Account", "definition": "An account with no KYC completion. Can receive money only. Cannot send or withdraw." },
      { "term": "Settlement", "definition": "The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation). Once settled, the transaction is irreversible." },
      { "term": "Sanctioned Entity", "definition": "An individual, organization, or jurisdiction appearing on a government-maintained sanctions list (e.g., OFAC, EU sanctions). Transfers to these entities must be blocked." },
      { "term": "Account Lock", "definition": "A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. The account becomes inaccessible until a support agent manually unlocks it." },
      { "term": "Daily Transfer Limit", "definition": "The maximum cumulative value of outbound transfers a user can initiate within a rolling or calendar day, determined by verification tier." },
      { "term": "Transaction Notification", "definition": "An alert sent to the user for any transaction exceeding $1,000 in value." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "An unverified account MUST NOT initiate a send or withdrawal transaction.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Allowing unverified users to move money out of the system violates KYC/AML regulations. This is a legal and data-integrity constraint — if violated, the platform is non-compliant.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to send or withdraw from an account with verification_status = 'unverified' must be rejected with an appropriate error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Unverified accounts CAN receive money.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Receiving funds does not create regulatory exposure for the platform. This is a state-integrity rule: the receive path must remain open regardless of KYC status to avoid blocking legitimate inbound flows.",
      "testable_assertion": "An inbound transfer to an account with verification_status = 'unverified' must succeed and credit the wallet balance."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $5,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $5,000 threshold is a business policy. A VP or compliance officer could raise or lower it based on risk appetite or regulatory changes. The enforcement mechanism is invariant; the value is not.",
      "testable_assertion": "A verified account that has already transferred $4,500 today must be rejected if it attempts to send $600 (cumulative $5,100 exceeds $5,000 limit)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Partially-verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $500.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Same reasoning as BR-3 — the threshold is policy, not physics. The concept of enforcing a lower limit for partial verification is sound, but the dollar amount is tunable.",
      "testable_assertion": "A partially-verified account that has already transferred $400 today must be rejected if it attempts to send $200 (cumulative $600 exceeds $500 limit)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "A transaction CANNOT be reversed once it has reached settled status (T+1).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Settlement finality is a core financial integrity constraint. Reversing a settled transaction would corrupt ledger state and violate counterparty trust. Disputes after settlement must follow a separate refund/chargeback process, not a reversal.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any reversal request against a transaction with status = 'settled' must be rejected. Only transactions with status = 'pending' (pre-settlement) may be reversed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Transfers to sanctioned entities MUST be blocked before execution.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a legal/regulatory hard constraint (OFAC, AML). Allowing even one transfer to a sanctioned entity exposes the platform to criminal penalties, fines, and license revocation. The system is corrupt if this is violated.",
      "testable_assertion": "A transfer where the recipient matches a sanctioned entity list must be blocked, the transaction must not be created, and the event must be logged for compliance audit."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Five consecutive failed login attempts MUST lock the account. Only a support agent can unlock it.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Account lock is a security integrity constraint. If the system allows a 6th attempt without locking, it is in a corrupt security state. The unlock-by-support-only rule prevents automated bypass of the security control.",
      "testable_assertion": "After 5 consecutive failed logins, the account status must be 'locked'. A subsequent valid login attempt must be rejected. The account must remain locked until a support agent explicitly unlocks it."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "A notification must be sent for any transaction exceeding $1,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The notification threshold is a business policy / UX preference. The $1,000 value and the notification channel could change without corrupting system state. Failure to notify is a degraded experience, not data corruption.",
      "testable_assertion": "A transaction of $1,001 must trigger a notification to the account holder. A transaction of $999 must not trigger a notification under this rule."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A user with an unverified account and a wallet balance of $200",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $100 to another user",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required, and the wallet balance remains $200"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A user with an unverified account",
      "when": "Another user sends them $500",
      "then": "The transfer succeeds and the recipient's wallet balance increases by $500"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A verified account that has sent $4,800 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $200",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because the cumulative daily total ($5,000) would equal the limit — OR it succeeds if the limit is inclusive. CLARIFICATION NEEDED: is the limit 'up to' or 'up to and including' $5,000?"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A verified account that has sent $4,800 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $300 (cumulative $5,100)",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected with an error indicating the daily transfer limit has been exceeded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A partially-verified account that has sent $0 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $500",
      "then": "The transaction succeeds (assuming $500 is at-or-under limit)"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A transaction initiated yesterday that has now reached T+1 settlement",
      "when": "The sender requests a reversal",
      "then": "The reversal is rejected with a message indicating the transaction is settled and irreversible"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A transaction initiated today that has not yet settled (status = 'pending')",
      "when": "The sender requests a reversal",
      "then": "The reversal is processed and the funds are returned to the sender's wallet"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A verified user attempting to send $2,000 to an entity on the sanctions list",
      "when": "The transfer is initiated",
      "then": "The transfer is blocked before any funds move, an audit log entry is created, and the user is informed the transfer cannot be completed"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A user who has entered the wrong password 4 times consecutively",
      "when": "They enter the wrong password a 5th time",
      "then": "The account is immediately locked, the user is informed, and subsequent login attempts (even with the correct password) are rejected until a support agent unlocks the account"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A user who failed 4 times, then succeeds on the 5th attempt",
      "when": "They log in successfully",
      "then": "The failed attempt counter resets to 0 and the account is NOT locked"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A verified user sends $1,500 to another user",
      "when": "The transaction is created",
      "then": "A notification is sent to the account holder informing them of the transaction"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A verified user sends $800 to another user",
      "when": "The transaction is created",
      "then": "No transaction-amount notification is triggered under the $1,000 rule"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "An unverified account with a balance of $1,000",
      "when": "The user attempts to withdraw $500",
      "then": "The withdrawal is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A partially-verified user attempts to withdraw funds", "expected_behavior": "The spec says unverified accounts cannot withdraw but is silent on partially-verified withdrawal rights. This needs clarification. Conservatively, partially-verified should also be blocked from withdrawals until full KYC." },
    { "scenario": "A transfer of exactly $1,000", "expected_behavior": "The spec says 'over $1,000' — a transaction of exactly $1,000 should NOT trigger a notification. Only $1,000.01+ should." },
    { "scenario": "A transfer of exactly $5,000 by a verified account with $0 sent today", "expected_behavior": "Ambiguous whether the limit is inclusive or exclusive. Needs clarification — does 'limit of $5,000' mean you can send up to and including $5,000, or up to $4,999.99?" },
    { "scenario": "User fails login 3 times, succeeds, then fails 5 more times", "expected_behavior": "The counter must reset on successful login. Only 5 consecutive failures trigger a lock. So the second streak of 5 should lock the account." },
    { "scenario": "Sanctions list is updated after a transfer is initiated but before settlement", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address mid-flight sanctions screening. Best practice: re-screen at settlement time. This is a compliance gap that needs product/legal clarification." },
    { "scenario": "A user sends $600 in two $300 transactions on a partially-verified account", "expected_behavior": "The daily limit applies cumulatively. The second $300 transaction should be rejected because $300 + $300 = $600 exceeds the $500 limit. The limit check must be against the rolling daily total, not per-transaction." },
    { "scenario": "A transaction to a sanctioned entity for $0.01", "expected_behavior": "The sanctions block applies regardless of amount. Even a zero-value or micro-transaction to a sanctioned entity must be blocked." },
    { "scenario": "Locked account receives an inbound transfer", "expected_behavior": "Spec does not address this. A locked account should still be able to receive funds — the lock is a login/access control, not a wallet freeze. Needs confirmation." },
    { "scenario": "Notification delivery fails (e.g., email bounce, push not delivered)", "expected_behavior": "Spec requires notification be 'sent' but does not define delivery guarantees or retry policy. The system should log the send attempt; whether retry is required needs clarification." },
    { "scenario": "What defines 'daily' for transfer limits — calendar day (which timezone?) or rolling 24h?", "expected_behavior": "Spec says 'daily' without defining the window. This must be clarified: UTC calendar day, user-local calendar day, or rolling 24-hour window. Each has different compliance and UX implications." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Incorrect limit enforcement could expose the platform to excess fraud loss. Sanctions violations carry fines from $50K to $10M+ per incident (OFAC). Over-restrictive limits reduce transaction volume and revenue. Settlement irreversibility protects against double-spend scenarios that would directly impact the platform's treasury.",
    "operational": "Account locking creates support ticket volume — support team staffing must account for unlock requests. Sanctions screening requires ongoing list synchronization (OFAC updates weekly). Daily limit tracking requires reliable, low-latency aggregation infrastructure. KYC verification pipeline throughput directly impacts user activation rates.",
    "user": "Unverified users can receive but not spend — this creates a 'golden jail' that incentivizes KYC completion. The 5-attempt lockout must balance security against user frustration (legitimate users who forget passwords). The $1,000 notification threshold provides transparency but may cause alert fatigue for high-volume users.",
    "risk": "Primary risks: (1) Sanctions screening failure — existential regulatory risk. (2) KYC bypass — regulatory and fraud risk. (3) Settlement reversal bug — financial integrity risk, potential double-spend. (4) Daily limit bypass via race conditions — fraud risk. (5) Account lock bypass — security/fraud risk.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "End Users (Senders)", "impact": "Subject to verification gates, daily limits, and account security controls. Experience directly shaped by how gracefully rejections and locks are handled." },
      { "group": "End Users (Recipients)", "impact": "Can receive funds regardless of their own verification status. May be confused if sender's transaction is blocked by sanctions or limits." },
      { "group": "Compliance / Legal Team", "impact": "Owns sanctions list updates, KYC policy definitions, and audit trail requirements. Directly liable if BR-1, BR-5, or BR-6 are violated." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Handles account unlock requests and post-settlement dispute escalations. Volume directly driven by lockout frequency and reversal denial inquiries." },
      { "group": "Finance / Treasury", "impact": "Settlement finality (BR-5) is critical for cash flow management and ledger reconciliation. Limit enforcement protects against outsized exposure." },
      { "group": "Product / Growth", "impact": "KYC friction impacts conversion funnel. Partially-verified tier is a growth lever — higher limits incentivize full verification." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 55,
    "reasoning": "The spec clearly defines the core rules but leaves several operationally critical details ambiguous: (1) partially-verified withdrawal rights are not stated, (2) 'daily' limit window is undefined, (3) inclusive vs exclusive limit boundaries are unspecified, (4) no mention of mid-flight sanctions re-screening, (5) notification delivery guarantees are absent. These gaps are not trivial — they affect compliance, UX, and financial correctness. Confidence is capped at 55 due to these ambiguities.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Unverified accounts cannot send or withdraw (BR-1) — unambiguous",
      "Settlement finality at T+1 (BR-5) — clear and standard",
      "Sanctions blocking is mandatory (BR-6) — regulatory hard stop",
      "5 consecutive failed logins lock the account (BR-7) — clear trigger and resolution",
      "Notification threshold is over $1,000 (BR-8) — clear boundary"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw",
      "Definition of 'daily' for transfer limit windows (timezone, rolling vs calendar)",
      "Inclusive vs exclusive boundary on daily limits ($5,000 exactly: allowed or rejected?)",
      "Mid-flight sanctions screening at settlement time",
      "Notification delivery guarantees and retry policy",
      "Whether a locked account can still receive inbound transfers"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Partially-verified accounts exist as a distinct tier between unverified and verified — the spec implies this by assigning them a $500 limit",
      "Daily transfer limits apply cumulatively across all transactions in the window, not per-transaction",
      "The failed login counter resets on a successful login (standard security pattern, not explicitly stated)",
      "Sanctions screening occurs at transaction initiation time (pre-execution), not only at settlement",
      "T+1 refers to one business day, following standard financial settlement conventions"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec defines withdrawal restrictions for 'unverified' accounts but does not address whether 'partially-verified' accounts can withdraw. Given the three-tier model (unverified / partially-verified / verified), this is a gap with compliance implications.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Compliance / Legal Team", "End Users (Partially-Verified)", "Product / Growth"],
      "options": ["A: Partially-verified can withdraw up to their $500 daily limit", "B: Partially-verified cannot withdraw (same restriction as unverified)", "C: Partially-verified can withdraw with additional step-up verification"],
      "recommendation": "Option B (block withdrawals for partially-verified) — conservative default that aligns with the KYC-first intent. Withdrawals create the highest regulatory exposure and should require full verification."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "'Daily transfer limit' does not define the time window. Calendar day (in what timezone?) or rolling 24-hour window? This affects limit enforcement correctness and user expectations.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["End Users (Senders)", "Finance / Treasury", "Compliance / Legal Team"],
      "options": ["A: UTC calendar day (midnight-to-midnight UTC)", "B: User-local calendar day based on account timezone", "C: Rolling 24-hour window from first transaction"],
      "recommendation": "Option A (UTC calendar day) — simplest to implement consistently, standard in fintech, avoids timezone manipulation exploits. Document clearly for users."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Sanctions list synchronization frequency and mid-flight screening are not specified. If a recipient is added to the sanctions list after a transfer is initiated but before T+1 settlement, should the transfer be blocked at settlement?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Compliance / Legal Team", "Finance / Treasury"],
      "options": ["A: Screen only at initiation time", "B: Screen at both initiation and settlement", "C: Screen at initiation, settlement, and on every sanctions list update for pending transactions"],
      "recommendation": "Option C — most compliant. OFAC guidance expects ongoing screening. This is a legal question and should be confirmed with counsel before implementation."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
